source_pr.title: test: stable the test case
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #16680 

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:
Remove the time interval check in the test.

How it Works:

### Related changes



### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test


Side effects


### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->
- No release note

symptoms: The test asserted an operation returns within a strict wall-clock budget (50ms), which can be violated under CI load.
failure_signature: Wall-clock assertion too strict: `elapsed < 50*time.Millisecond` intermittently fails under load
root_cause_explanation: This test used a strict wall-clock assertion (`elapsed < 50*time.Millisecond`) to validate a lock acquisition path returns “immediately”. Under high CPU load or slow CI, the operation can legitimately take longer than 50ms even if the behavior is correct, making the test flaky. This PR stabilizes the test by removing the time-interval assertion and only asserting the expected error behavior.
fix_pattern: Avoid strict wall-clock assertions in tests; assert on functional behavior (errors/results) instead of requiring an operation to finish within a tiny time window.

evidence: evidence: PR title: test: stable the test case
evidence: remove time-based assertion `elapsed < 50*time.Millisecond` to avoid timing flake (store/tikv/2pc_test.go)
